name: South Africa
d12Details: Mature armed response (ADT, Fidelity, G4S), active CSIRT, comprehensive security regulation, international insurance available.
dmRationale: Mature ecosystem, hyperscaler presence, ISO-certified DCs, funded national AI strategy

name: Nigeria
d12Details: Very high organised crime and armed robbery risk in industrial zones. ngCERT active but inconsistent. Lagos DCs must budget $5-10M in proprietary security.
dmRationale: National AI strategy, data protection law, growing hyperscaler footprint

name: Egypt
d12Details: Strong physical security culture but incomplete cyber CERT and bureaucratic emergency response.
dmRationale: National AI strategy, data protection law, growing hyperscaler footprint

name: Morocco
d12Details: Low crime, stable governance, growing CERT capability in Casablanca industrial corridor.
dmRationale: National AI strategy, data protection law, growing hyperscaler footprint

name: Algeria

dataProtection.label: Draft DP law — not yet enacted

d12Details: Moderate security environment. Limited CERT development.
dmRationale: Strategy present, infrastructure developing

name: Kenya
d12Details: Functional armed response within 10-15 minutes, improving CERT, rising investor confidence.
dmRationale: National AI strategy, data protection law, growing hyperscaler footprint

name: Rwanda
d12Details: Lowest property crime in East Africa. Presidential Guard proximity. Active RISA CERT. D12=5 makes Kigali the recommended EAC shared hub host.
dmRationale: Kigali Innovation City, EAC shared compute hub candidate, strong AI/digital strategy, highest D12 in East Africa

name: Angola
d12Details: Limited security infrastructure. Very low electricity tariff advantage.
dmRationale: Angola Cables (SACS, MONET), ultra-low electricity tariff, oil revenue diversification into digital

name: Ghana
d12Details: Functional security with moderate gaps. Low crime relative to West Africa.
dmRationale: Strategy present, infrastructure developing

name: Tanzania
d12Details: Functional security environment. Moderate organised crime risk.
dmRationale: Strategy present, infrastructure developing

name: Senegal
d12Details: Moderate security. Functional basic services.
dmRationale: Diamniadio Digital Park, emerging tech hub, Senegal Numerique 2025 strategy, submarine cable hub

name: Uganda
d12Details: Functional security with notable gaps. Moderate crime.
dmRationale: National 4IR Strategy, Data Protection Act 2019, growing ICT sector, EAC member

name: Tunisia
d12Details: Moderate security environment with functional but limited services.
dmRationale: Strategy present, infrastructure developing

name: Ethiopia
d12Details: Post-Tigray conflict legacy creates residual political and operational security risk. Ethio Telecom infrastructure was targeted.
dmRationale: Strategy present, infrastructure developing

name: Cote d'Ivoire
d12Details: Functional security with moderate gaps. West African organised crime risk during construction.
dmRationale: Strategy present, infrastructure developing

name: Mauritius
d12Details: Best regulatory environment in Africa. Financial hub. SAFE/LION submarine cables. Small market but high per-capita demand.
dmRationale: Financial hub, GDPR-equivalent data protection, Cybercity Ebene, AI strategy 2023, submarine cable nexus

name: Cameroon
d12Details: Uncertain governance environment. Moderate organised crime risk.
dmRationale: CEMAC's largest economy, Kribi submarine cable, emerging digital strategy

name: Libya
d12Details: Divided governance between east and west. Armed militias control key infrastructure. Near-uninsurable.
dmRationale: Oil wealth potential, but conflict prevents deployment

name: Zimbabwe

dataProtection.label: Cyber and Data Protection Act 2021 — early

d12Details: Challenging security environment. Currency instability compounds risk.
dmRationale: ICT roadmap exists but economic instability limits investment

name: Zambia
d12Details: Moderate security environment. Reasonable for sub-20MW demand.
dmRationale: Smart Zambia programme, Data Protection Act 2021, cheap hydro power from Kariba/Kafue

name: Gabon
d12Details: Post-coup uncertainty. Limited security services.
dmRationale: High GDP per capita for Central Africa, Nkok SEZ, post-coup digital reform ambitions

name: DR Congo
d12Details: Highest security risk on the continent. Active conflict, no CERT, near-uninsurable.
dmRationale: 100M+ population potential, but conflict and infrastructure prevent realization

name: Botswana
d12Details: Very stable, low crime. Strong governance. Too small for standalone.
dmRationale: Moderate readiness, small market

name: Namibia
d12Details: Low crime, stable governance. Limited scale for standalone DC.
dmRationale: Good governance, small population limits demand. Digital transformation strategy exists.

name: Sudan
d12Details: Active civil war. Infrastructure severely damaged.
dmRationale: Civil war destroyed infrastructure. Demand deferred until peace.

name: Mozambique
d12Details: Insurgency risk in north. Southern Maputo corridor is more stable.
dmRationale: LNG revenues growing, Maputo corridor near South Africa, cable landings

name: Djibouti
d12Details: Strategic cable hub location. High tariffs offset by connectivity.
dmRationale: Strategic cable hub (13+ submarine cables), military base presence drives connectivity

name: Benin
d12Details: Improving governance under Talon. Northern border insecurity from Sahel spillover.
dmRationale: Glo-Djigbé GDIZ, digital economy code, improving governance

name: Mauritania
d12Details: Bridge between North and West Africa. Limited infrastructure. Gas revenues growing.
dmRationale: Gas revenues could fund digital, Morocco gateway to West Africa

name: Guinea
d12Details: Military transitional government. Mineral wealth but weak digital infrastructure.
dmRationale: Nascent digital economy, military rule

name: Madagascar
d12Details: Island isolation adds logistics cost. Limited security infrastructure.
dmRationale: Island isolation, EASSy cable access, Francophone digital initiatives

name: Mali
d12Details: Active conflict in north. Military government. High risk.
dmRationale: Military government, ECOWAS suspended, demand deferred

name: Burkina Faso
d12Details: Active conflict. Military government. Very high risk.
dmRationale: Military government, security crisis, demand deferred

name: Malawi
d12Details: Low risk but very limited infrastructure.
dmRationale: Digital Malawi strategy, small economy, SADC member

name: Togo
d12Details: Small market. Moderate security. Shares grid with Ghana.
dmRationale: PIA Adétikopé, small ECOWAS market, Ghana sharing partner

name: Republic of Congo
d12Details: Oil economy. Twin city with Kinshasa across river. Limited infrastructure.
dmRationale: Oil economy, twin city with Kinshasa, ECCAS member

name: Equatorial Guinea
d12Details: Oil wealth but opaque governance. Small population. Spanish-speaking CEMAC member.
dmRationale: Oil wealth, minimal digital investment

name: Somalia
d12Details: Minimal infrastructure. Active conflict. Highest tariff on continent.
dmRationale: Active conflict. Mogadishu has some mobile-first digital, connects via Nairobi

name: Niger
d12Details: Military junta since July 2023. ECOWAS sanctions. Active jihadist threats in border regions.
dmRationale: Minimal digital infrastructure, military rule

name: Sierra Leone
d12Details: Post-conflict stability but very limited infrastructure. Power supply unreliable.
dmRationale: Minimal infrastructure

name: Liberia
d12Details: Post-conflict recovery. ACE submarine cable landing. Highest electricity tariff tier.
dmRationale: Minimal infrastructure

name: Cabo Verde
d12Details: Best governance in West Africa. Island nation, submarine cable hub. Small market limits standalone DC.
dmRationale: Excellent governance, submarine cable hub, tiny market

name: Chad
d12Details: Military transitional government. Boko Haram threat in Lake Chad basin. Landlocked.
dmRationale: Minimal infrastructure

name: South Sudan
d12Details: World's youngest nation. Post-civil war. Minimal infrastructure across all sectors.
dmRationale: Minimal infrastructure

name: Eswatini
d12Details: Tiny landlocked monarchy. Dependent on South African grid. Recent civil unrest.
dmRationale: Small market, dependent on South Africa

name: Gambia
d12Details: Small economy surrounded by Senegal. ACE cable landing.
dmRationale: Minimal infrastructure, small market

name: Burundi
d12Details: EAC member. Very limited infrastructure. Would benefit from Kigali shared hub.
dmRationale: Nascent digital economy, EAC member

name: Lesotho
d12Details: Landlocked within South Africa. Fully dependent on SA infrastructure. Cheap hydro power.
dmRationale: Small market, enclave of South Africa

name: Seychelles
d12Details: Tiny island economy. Excellent governance. SEAS cable. Tourism-dependent.
dmRationale: High governance, minimal market size

name: Guinea-Bissau
d12Details: Chronic political instability. Narco-state risk. Minimal digital infrastructure.
dmRationale: Minimal infrastructure

name: Central African Republic
d12Details: Active conflict. Russian paramilitary presence. Near-zero digital infrastructure.
dmRationale: Minimal infrastructure

name: Sao Tome and Principe
d12Details: Tiny island nation. Good governance for size. Submarine cable access.
dmRationale: Minimal market size

name: Eritrea
d12Details: Most isolated country in Africa. State controls all telecoms. No private DC feasible.
dmRationale: Closed economy, no private investment

name: Comoros
d12Details: Island archipelago. EAF submarine cable access. Very small market.
dmRationale: Minimal market size
